file_summary: This file provides JSON serialization utilities for the itsdangerous library, specifically a compact JSON formatter that removes unnecessary whitespace from JSON output while maintaining compatibility with Python's standard json module.

line_windows.summary: This window includes the module imports and the entire _CompactJSON class definition. Lines 1-3 contain imports of the json and typing modules with aliases. Lines 5-18 define the _CompactJSON class with its docstring and two static methods: loads (for parsing JSON) and dumps (for serializing objects to compact JSON format). The dumps method is configured to produce minimal whitespace output by default.

key_symbols: {'name': '_CompactJSON', 'type': 'class', 'description': 'Wrapper around the json module that strips whitespace from JSON output by using compact separators.'}
{'name': '_CompactJSON.loads', 'type': 'static_method', 'description': 'Static method that delegates to json.loads to parse JSON strings or bytes into Python objects.'}
{'name': '_CompactJSON.dumps', 'type': 'static_method', 'description': 'Static method that delegates to json.dumps with default settings for compact output (no whitespace, Unicode support).'}
dependencies_or_calls: {'name': 'json', 'type': 'standard_library_module', 'usage': "Imported as '_json' to avoid naming conflicts, used for JSON parsing and serialization."}
